req.title: Account-level transaction limits (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A customer shall configure daily per-channel limits within regulatory caps; changes take effect after a 2-hour cool-down. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-092 has four distinct concerns: (1) valid limit submission stores a pending record, (2) submission over the regulatory cap is rejected with 422, (3) boundary value at exactly the regulatory cap is accepted, (4) duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key returns the original result without side-effects, and (5) the 2-hour cool-down state transition determines when a limit becomes effective. Tests are mapped to EP (happy-path valid partition), NEG (over-cap rejection), BVA (at-cap boundary), STC (cool-down state machine), and DT (idempotency decision table).

tests.name: test_valid_limit_submission_returns_pending
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid JWT
Requested daily limit is below the regulatory cap
actions: Generate a unique Idempotency-Key
POST /accounts/{id}/limits with a valid per-channel daily limit payload
Assert HTTP 202 Accepted is returned
Assert response body contains status='pending'
Assert effective_at is approximately now + 2 hours
expected: effective_at is within [now+1h55m, now+2h5m] tolerance window
trace: R-FIN-092: valid limit stored as pending
R-FIN-092: changes take effect after 2-hour cool-down
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
import uuid

def test_valid_limit_submission_returns_pending(api_client, auth_headers, account_id, regulatory_cap):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "channel": "online",
        "daily_limit": regulatory_cap - 1
    }
    headers = {**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/limits",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 202, f"Expected 202, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending"

    effective_at = datetime.fromisoformat(body["effective_at"])
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    assert now + timedelta(hours=1, minutes=55) <= effective_at <= now + timedelta(hours=2, minutes=5)

tests.name: test_limit_over_regulatory_cap_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid JWT
Requested daily limit exceeds the regulatory cap by at least 1 unit
actions: Generate a unique Idempotency-Key
POST /accounts/{id}/limits with daily_limit = regulatory_cap + 1
Assert HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity is returned
Assert error body references 'regulatory_cap' violation
expected: Response body contains an error code referencing regulatory cap violation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_limit_over_regulatory_cap_returns_422(api_client, auth_headers, account_id, regulatory_cap):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "channel": "online",
        "daily_limit": regulatory_cap + 1
    }
    headers = {**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/limits",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    errors = body.get("errors", body.get("detail", ""))
    error_str = str(errors).lower()
    assert "regulatory" in error_str or "cap" in error_str or "limit" in error_str

tests.name: test_limit_at_exact_regulatory_cap_is_accepted
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid JWT
Requested daily limit equals exactly the regulatory cap
actions: Generate a unique Idempotency-Key
POST /accounts/{id}/limits with daily_limit = regulatory_cap (exact boundary)
Assert HTTP 202 Accepted is returned
Assert response body contains status='pending'
expected: No validation error for being at the cap
trace: R-FIN-092: daily per-channel limits within regulatory caps
R-FIN-092: 422 only when over cap, not at cap

tests.name: test_duplicate_idempotency_key_returns_original_result
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid JWT
Requested daily limit is below the regulatory cap
actions: Generate a single Idempotency-Key to be reused
POST /accounts/{id}/limits with the key — first submission
Assert HTTP 202 and capture response body + headers
POST /accounts/{id}/limits with the SAME key — duplicate submission
Assert second response is identical to the first (status code, body, limit_id)
expected: Both response bodies are identical (same limit_id, same status, same effective_at)
No duplicate pending record created in the system
trace: R-FIN-092: idempotent operation — duplicate Idempotency-Key must return original result
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_duplicate_idempotency_key_returns_original_result(api_client, auth_headers, account_id, regulatory_cap):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "channel": "mobile",
        "daily_limit": regulatory_cap - 100
    }
    headers = {**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    resp1 = api_client.post(f"/accounts/{account_id}/limits", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp1.status_code == 202, f"First call failed: {resp1.status_code} {resp1.text}"
    body1 = resp1.json()

    resp2 = api_client.post(f"/accounts/{account_id}/limits", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp2.status_code == 202, f"Duplicate call failed: {resp2.status_code} {resp2.text}"
    body2 = resp2.json()

    assert body1["limit_id"] == body2["limit_id"], "Idempotency violated: different limit_id returned"
    assert body1["status"] == body2["status"]
    assert body1["effective_at"] == body2["effective_at"]

tests.name: test_limit_not_effective_before_cooldown_expires
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid JWT
A limit change was submitted less than 2 hours ago (pending state)
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/limits to create a new pending limit
Immediately GET /accounts/{id}/limits to read current effective limit
Assert the newly submitted limit is in status='pending', not 'active'
Assert the currently active limit is unchanged from the pre-submission value
expected: Existing active limit is unchanged (pre-cool-down state preserved)
effective_at is in the future (>= now + 2 hours)
trace: R-FIN-092: changes take effect after a 2-hour cool-down
R-FIN-092: pending limit stored; effective after cool-down
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone

def test_limit_not_effective_before_cooldown_expires(api_client, auth_headers, account_id, regulatory_cap, current_active_limit):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    new_limit_value = current_active_limit - 50 if current_active_limit > 50 else current_active_limit + 50
    payload = {
        "channel": "online",
        "daily_limit": min(new_limit_value, regulatory_cap)
    }
    headers = {**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    post_resp = api_client.post(f"/accounts/{account_id}/limits", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert post_resp.status_code == 202
    pending = post_resp.json()
    assert pending["status"] == "pending"

    get_resp = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}/limits", headers=auth_headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    active_limits = get_resp.json().get("active", [])
    online_active = next((l for l in active_limits if l["channel"] == "online"), None)

    if online_active:
        assert online_active["daily_limit"] == current_active_limit, "Active limit mutated before cool-down"

    effective_at = datetime.fromisoformat(pending["effective_at"])
    assert effective_at > datetime.now(timezone.utc), "effective_at must be in the future"
